text: Organizational Meeting of the 47th Session of the Human Rights Council
The meeting was presided by H.E. Ms. Nazhat Shameem Khan, President of the UN Human Rights
Council. The main objectives of the meeting:
1. Review the draft Programme of Work (living document, available om Extranet)
3. Share information on draft resolutions and other initiatives
A new Vice-President of the Council from the African Group, H.E. Ms. Ebyan Mahamed Salah (Somalia),
was elected in order to replace H.E. Mr. Ali Ibn Abi Talib Abdelrahman Mahmoud who, for personal
reasons, had to stop performing his duties.
The 47th session is scheduled to take place from 21 June to 15 July 2021. For the first time, the June
session will continue into its 4th week due to the fact that new mandates were created, such as
Interactive Dialogues and panel discussions, that require additional meeting hours.
The online system of inscription for the list of speakers for all IDs and Panel discussions will open
on Tuesday, 15 June, at 4 pm and will close 24 hours before the meeting in which the debate is
scheduled to start. After this, delegations can still inscribe or withdraw by sending an email to
- The online system of inscriptions for the adoption of the UPR outcomes will open on Monday, 21
June, at 9 am and will close on Thursday, 24 June, at 6 pm. No inscriptions will be possible after
- For panel debates: states will need to inscribe ranking the panels in order of priorities.
- The deadline for draft proposals to be considered is Friday, 2 July, at 1 pm.
- the list of speakers for all debates will open online on Thursday, 17 June, at 2 pm.
7 panel discussion will be held during the 47th sessions:
- High-level panel discussion on the multisectoral prevention of and response to female genital
mutilation (23 June 2021, 10 am-12 pm) – introduced by Cameroon on behalf of the group of African
states. Confirmed participation of H.E. Mr. Roch Marc Christian Kaboré, President of Burkina Faso.
- Panel discussion on the tenth anniversary of the Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
- Panel discussion on the human rights of older persons in the context of climate change (30 June
2021, 10 am-12 pm) – introduced by Viet Nam.
- Annual full-day discussion on the human rights of women:
Panel 1: Violence against women and girls with disabilities (5 July 2021, 10 am-12 pm) – o
Panel 2: Gender-equal socioeconomic recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic (6 July 2021, o
- Quadrennial panel discussion on promoting human rights through sport and the Olympic ideal (7
July 2021, 10 am-12 pm) – introduced by Greece on behalf of the main sponsors1. Theme: The
potential of leveraging sport and the Olympic ideal for promoting human rights for young people.
- Annual thematic panel discussion on technical cooperation and capacity-building – introduced by
Thailand, on behalf of the main sponsors. Theme: Technical cooperation to advance the right to
education and ensure inclusive and equitable quality education and lifelong learning for all.
- The session will begin with the presentation of the Annual Report by the High Commissioner on
- No general debate will be held during the 47th session. The reports of the High Commissioner,
Secretary General and the Subsidiary bodies will be considered during the relevant debates of the
- The Council will hold 25 interactive dialogues (IDs) with special procedure mandate holders and
mechanisms, 6 interactive dialogues with the High Commissioner and 1 interactive dialogue with
the Special Advisor to the Secretary-General.
- The Council will adopt 14 outcomes of the UPR and elect 7 mandate holders of Special Procedures,
including the unforeseen vacancy of the Independent Expert on foreign debt. The concerned regional
groups should appoint the members of the Consultative Group without delay.
The three-year Programme of Work (2021-2023) has recently been updated and posted on Extranet.
The voluntary yearly Calendar for thematic resolutions is regularly updated by the Bureau before each
Council section. The updates Calendar will be posted on Extranet prior to the 47th session. As far as the
country resolutions are concerned, the delegations should share the initiatives to ensure transparency
and predictability of the Council’s work.
- Cameroon, on behalf of the African Group: menstrual hygiene management, human rights and
gender equality, elimination of harmful practice of accusation of malicious use of witchcraft.
- Viet Nam on behalf the Core Group2: human rights and climate change, with the focus on all persons
- Ukraine: cooperation with and assistance to Ukraine in the field of human rights.
- Pakistan on behalf of the OIC: women rights of Muslim minorities in Myanmar.
- Brazil on behalf of the Core group3: human rights in the context of HIV and AIDS.
- United Kingdom on behalf of the Syria Core Group4: the situation of human rights in Syrian Arab
Republic with the thematic focus on the missing people.
- European Union: renewal of the mandate of the Special Rapporteurs on Belarus and Eritrea.

3 Portugal, Mozambique, Thailand, Colombia and Brazil
4 France, Germany, Italy, Jordan, Kuwait, Netherlands, Qatar, Turkey, United Kingdom, United States

- Azerbaijan: enhancement of international cooperation in the field of human rights.
- Republic of Korea on behalf of the Core group6: new and emerging digital technologies and human
- Mexico: human rights and migrants, with a focus on vulnerable migrants.
- Ecuador together with Peru: impact of arm transfers on human rights.
- Ireland on behalf of the Cross-Regional Core group7: civil society space.
- United Arab Emirates with United Kingdom: realizing the right to equal education by every girl.
- Estonia on behalf of the Core group8: preventable maternal mortality and morbidity.
- Sweden on behalf of the Core group9: human rights on Internet.
- Canada: accelerating efforts on eliminating all forms of violence against women and girls, with a
specific focus on women and girls with disabilities.
- 8 June: virtual side event on the detention centre “Isolation” in Donetsk (Ukraine).
- 15 June: high-level panel on new and emerging technologies (Austria and the Republic of Korea
together with Brazil, Denmark, Morocco, Singapore, and the OHCHR).
- 23 June: side event on the MeToo movement; side event on the transgender violence (United States)
- 24 June: side events on human rights in digital era (Switzerland).
- 29 June: side event on sustainable territorial development (Morocco).
- 29 June: virtual event on the representation of women in the human rights bodies and mechanisms
- 2 July: Strengthening the role of the UN system on drug policy (Switzerland).
- 5 July: Recent developments on peaceful assembly and demonstrations (Switzerland).
- 7 July: Workshop on the legal aspects of recognizing of the right to environment (Costa Rica together
with Morocco, Slovenia, and the Geneva Academy).
- “Economic violence is violence: helping women to get back on their feet” (Israel, together with
Argentina, Germany, Kenya, UN Women, an NGO “Women’s spirit”).
- High-level panel discussion on new and emerging technologies (Denmark).
- Sustainable recovery pledge (Denmark and the Core-Group)
- Joint statement on the negative impact of disinformation on human rights as a part of the ID with
Special Rapporteur on freedom of expression Donetsk (Ukraine).
- The 47th session of the Human Rights Council is scheduled to take place in Room XVIII.
- State delegations are strongly encouraged to send pre-recorded video statements or deliver their
statements through live link via the Zoom platform.
5 Austria, Argentina, Brazil, Etiopia, Indonesia, Poland, United Kingdom, Morocco
6 Austria, Brazil, Denmark, Morocco, Singapore, Republic of Korea
- State delegations who face technical difficulties, as well as countries concerned with a reports or
dialogue, could exceptionally deliver their statements from Room XVIII.
- Delegations can raise points of order without being in the room by raising hand in Zoom.
- Delegations would be able to exercise their rights of reply remotely, in the same manner as during
the 46th regular session, by sending a request to hrclistofspeakers@ohchr.org.
- Special procedures mandate holders, members of investigative mechanisms and panellists who are
unable to travel to Geneva would be strongly encouraged to send pre-recorded video messages and
participate in interactive dialogues or panel discussions via Zoom.
- NGOs are invited to submit pre-recorded video statements for interactive dialogues, panels and UPR
adoptions. The full official name of the NGO should be displayed within the video.
- Side events cannot take place inside the Palais des Nations. In case NGOs decide to organize virtual
events, information on such events could be listed on the OHCHR civil society webpage along with a
statement indicating that the events are not official Council events.
- Delegations would be invited to organize their informal consultations and group meetings virtually
using the WebEx platform. The representatives of the members of the core group of a draft resolution
would have the opportunity to lead the virtual informal consultations from Room XXVI or another
- Delegations wishing to organize virtual informal consultations and group meetings on the UNOG-
provided WebEx platform should contact the relevant focal point within the Council secretariat (Mini
- The same health and safety measures that were applied during the 46th regular session of the Human
Rights Council would continue to be applied in a very strict manner.
- These modalities would apply exclusively to the 47th regular session during the period when COVID-
19 restrictions prohibit in-person meetings with more than 100 participants and would not serve as
Taking into consideration the development of the COVID-19 situation and the Swiss regulations between
now and 13 July 2021, the Bureau agreed to keep under consideration the modalities for voting and to
reconvene to discuss this issue with the view to making a proposal at the end of June.
The Bureau underscored that the proposed extraordinary modalities for the 47th session would be
revisited should the host country and UNOG restrictions be further eased to the extent that it would be
possible for at least one delegate per Member and Observer State as well as a number of International
Organizations and NHRI/NGO representatives to attend the session.
H.E. Ms. Keva Lorraine Bain. is serving as a Chair of the Accessibility Task Force. There is a focal point on
accessibility in the Secretary that can be contacted if necessary.
A need to maintain the climate of trust and safety, avoiding any act of intimidation or reprisals, against
individual or groups who cooperate or have cooperated with human rights mechanisms at the UN.
Out of 64 reports to be presented during the 47th session, 38 reports are available in all languages and
20 are available in some languages and will be posted in the coming weeks.
Full video-recording of the Organizational Session (7 June 2021): UN WebTV
